headline: Bilaterals III parliamentary review underway
summary: — Two Federal Assembly committees (SPK-N/AFE-N) began formal examination of the Bilaterals III dispatch submitted by the Federal Council on 13 March 2026.

headline: Swiss data-protection conference recommends ban on US hyperscaler clouds for public sector
summary: — Privatim, the cantonal data-protection authority network, concluded that government agencies routing professional-secrecy data (medical, tax, social security, education records) through Microsoft 365, AWS, or Google Workspace are acting unconstitutionally under the US CLOUD Act.

headline: Geneva Airport access restricted for Evian G7 (15–17 June)
summary: — Several GVA car parks will close and security will be reinforced while the G7 Leaders' Summit takes place across the border in Evian-les-Bains.

headline: National Council demands stricter medical certificate standards
summary: — Switzerland's lower house voted to impose tighter requirements on physicians issuing absence certificates for employees.

headline: Swisscom Threat Radar 2026: AI and supply-chain risk now strategic
summary: — Released 27 April, Swisscom's annual Swiss-market threat assessment elevates four strategic risk areas: AI-augmented attacks, software supply-chain compromise, digital sovereignty (data localisation), and OT/IT convergence in critical infrastructure.

headline: Iran-US mutual Hormuz blockade — Tehran's 14-point proposal awaits Trump
summary: — Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has been blocked since 28 February 2026, when US and Israeli strikes killed Supreme Leader Khamenei and the IRGC closed the strait.

headline: Gaza ceasefire fragile; UN Security Council open debate (21 May)
summary: — Deputy Special Coordinator Nickolay Mladenov told the Security Council that every ceasefire violation risks "unravelling" the agreement; IDF logged 19 violations between 21 April and 5 May.

headline: Trump-Xi summit aftermath: "board of trade," $17 bn agriculture, Taiwan warning
summary: — Following the 14–15 May Beijing summit, the US and China agreed to establish a "board of trade" and a "board of investment" to manage bilateral economic relations.

headline: Russia-Ukraine: first net Russian territorial loss in nearly two years
summary: — In the four weeks ending 19 May 2026, Russian forces suffered a net loss of 69 square miles — the first such reversal since mid-2024 according to Russia Matters' weekly accounting.

headline: India-Pakistan ceasefire completes one year
summary: — The US-brokered ceasefire reached its first anniversary on 10 May 2026.

headline: CISA adds 7 KEV entries (20 May) — two 2026 Microsoft Defender flaws
summary: — CISA updated its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on 20 May with seven entries confirmed under active exploitation.

headline: CVE-2026-42945 — CVSS 9.2 NGINX heap overflow under active exploitation
summary: — A heap buffer overflow in ngx_http_rewrite_module affects NGINX versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0 (an 18-year-old flaw).

headline: nginx-poolslip — unauthenticated RCE 0-day in NGINX 1.31.0, no CVE, no patch
summary: — Researcher Vega of NebSec disclosed on 21 May a zero-day that bypasses ASLR to achieve full unauthenticated remote code execution on NGINX 1.31.0.

headline: TanStack supply-chain attack (TeamPCP / "Mini Shai-Hulud") hits OpenAI, Mistral AI, Grafana
summary: — On 11 May 2026, threat actor group TeamPCP hijacked TanStack's CI/CD OIDC identity mid-workflow, publishing 84 malicious artifacts across 42 @tanstack npm packages in six minutes.

headline: Drupal CVE-2026-9082 — "highly critical" SQL injection / RCE on PostgreSQL sites
summary: — A vulnerability in Drupal Core's database-abstraction API (ironically used to sanitise SQL) allows unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary SQL on PostgreSQL backends, enabling RCE, privilege escalation, and information disclosure.
